plan.title: Drop stale clock-synced triggers after wake

narratives.Overview: After the machine wakes from sleep, time announcements and other scheduled sounds fire as a backlog of missed triggers, announcing times that are already past until the schedule catches up to now. Every scheduler maps a wall-clock instant onto the audio clock and enqueues ahead; a suspend stalls the audio clock while wall time runs on, so the mapping — and any queued schedule — points at a civil time that has passed. Announce resync is wired only to visibilitychange / AudioContext statechange, neither of which necessarily fires on wake, and the meditation / break / chord schedulers ring an unbounded-staleness catch-up whenever their schedule falls behind.
narratives.Requirements: FR-1: Detect a suspend/resume by watching wall-clock and audio-clock divergence while the context runs. FR-2: On wake, re-anchor every scheduler to the live clocks and cancel schedules mapped from the stale clock pair. FR-3: A schedule stale beyond a bounded grace drops its catch-up fire instead of playing it. FR-4: Announcements never speak a boundary missed during the suspend. NFR-1: A throttled background tab keeps its existing catch-up behavior — the wake path must not trigger on timer throttling alone. NFR-2: No regression of #47 lookahead, #62 continual-loop, or #73 double-fire fixes.

items.title: Pure wake detection + shared catch-up staleness bound
Bound stale catch-up in meditation / break / chord schedulers
Engine resync contract; announce drops boundaries missed while suspended
